alerts.llm_explanation:
**PART C: Investigator Narrative**
A hub-and-spoke mule network pattern has been identified. A central hub account exhibits both high in-degree (aggregation) and high out-degree (disbursement) within a short time window, characteristic of a money-mule coordinator account. Risk score: 85.5/100.

**Recommendation:** Escalate to senior compliance officer. File CTR and STR for all connected accounts. Refer to law enforcement under PMLA Section 13.

**PART C: Investigator Narrative**
A hub-and-spoke mule network pattern has been identified. A central hub account exhibits both high in-degree (aggregation) and high out-degree (disbursement) within a short time window, characteristic of a money-mule coordinator account. Risk score: 82.69/100.

**Recommendation:** Escalate to senior compliance officer. File CTR and STR for all connected accounts. Refer to law enforcement under PMLA Section 13.

**PART C: Investigator Narrative**
A directed cyclic fund flow has been detected, consistent with round-tripping used to obscure beneficial ownership. Funds originating from the cycle's entry node were routed through intermediary accounts and returned to an associated account, forming a closed loop. The system assigned a risk score of 80.75/100.

**Recommendation:** Freeze all accounts in the cycle and initiate Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) under Section 12 of PMLA. File STR with FIU-IND within 7 days.

**PART C: Investigator Narrative**
A high fan-in structuring pattern (smurfing) has been identified. Multiple distinct source accounts transferred amounts clustered just below the ₹50,000 Cash Transaction Report threshold, converging on a single beneficiary. This is consistent with structuring designed to avoid CTR triggers. Risk score: 66.0/100.

**Recommendation:** Immediately file a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR). Freeze the beneficiary account and initiate KYC re-verification on all sources.

**Recommendation:** Escalate to senior compliance officer. File CTR and STR for all connected accounts. Refer to law enforcement under PMLA Section 13.

**PART C: Investigator Narrative**
A high fan-in structuring pattern (smurfing) has been identified. Multiple distinct source accounts transferred amounts clustered just below the ₹50,000 Cash Transaction Report threshold, converging on a single beneficiary. This is consistent with structuring designed to avoid CTR triggers. Risk score: 62.56/100.

**Recommendation:** Immediately file a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR). Freeze the beneficiary account and initiate KYC re-verification on all sources.
